master_row.title: Rho Dark Apparent Relation
master_row.canonical_statement: \begin{theorem}[Discrete Dark Matter Distribution] In $S^3$ topology with radius $R_U$, black hole accumulation at discrete positions yields apparent dark matter density: \begin{equation} \rho_{\text{dark}}^{\text{apparent}} = \frac{c^2}{4\pi GR_U^2} \Delta_{S^3}\Phi_{\text{dark}} \end{equation} where $\Phi_{\text{dark}}(\chi_{\text{obs}}) = -G\sum_i \frac{M_i}{R_U\sin(|\chi_{\text{obs}} - \chi_i|)}$ \end{theorem}
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Antipodal Dark Matter’.
master_row.dictionary: Rho Dark Apparent Relation — ρ_dark^apparent = (c^2)/(4π GR_U^2) Δ_S^3Φ_dark
master_row.encyclopedia: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `00AllEdit\analysis_all_edit_focus_11_22\output\all_edit_equation_arc_compact.csv` for registry alignment and later derivation.
